text:
BBCfans weren’t happy with the ongoing coverage of this year’sWinter Olympics, currently taking place in Italy, as they were forced to switch off partway through a segment. Viewers of the cross country event were left furious as the feed suddenly cut, 40km into a 50km race. It switched fromiPlayerto live on BBC Two, but some viewers fumed they weren’t informed of the change.

One fuming viewer tweeted: “Hugest congrats to the @BBCSport @BBCiPlayer who covered 40km of the 50km cross country this morning and then just decided they couldn’t be bothered to show the finish. WTF? It can’t be that difficult to start and finish one race/event on an iPlayer channel.

“I was watching on my phone in the car and suddenly it’s just not there anymore. I don’t want to switch to BBC2 or TNT. That’s not the point.”

Read more:'I'm going' – Reform's Laila Cunningham storms off air as migrant row explodes

Read more:'I took famous picture of Andrew after arrest and one thing left me speechless'

Someone reached out to defend theBBC, writing in response: “They switched it from iPlayer to BBC 2, as they have been doing all the way through the Olympics.They even say what they are doing!”

But the original poster clapped back: “Not for at least 10 mins they didn’t. I was between TNT, iPlayer and BBC2 in the car swearing like a native. So annoying.”

Another echoed: “They’ve done it yet again. Every bloody event near enough. The worst coverage of the Winter Olympics ever.”

“I saw it too, but what’s worse is something starts on one channel, then they switch to another channel, then switch back to the channel it was on in the first place!” somebody else moaned.

It comes after commentator John Hunt had to apologise on airafter a mistake filming Team GB athletes Matt Weston and Tabby Stoecker taking gold in the mixed team skeleton relay event last week.

We use your sign-up to provide content in ways you've consented to and to improve our understanding of you. This may include adverts from us and 3rd parties based on our understanding. You can unsubscribe at any time. Read ourPrivacy Policy

The camera was instead focused on Weston’s coach rather than the moment he pushed off to sprint across the ice, and missed his start completely. The broadcast wasn’t controlled by the Beeb, rather being part of the official Olympics broadcast, but host John told viewers at home: “Oh, what are they doing?

“What are they playing at? That’s terrible. That’s a great start. Sorry about the pictures but Matt is on his way.”

title: BBC Olympics viewers switch off immediately as they fume ‘worst coverage ever' | TV & Radio | Showbiz & TV | Express.co.uk
author: Jess Phillips
description: BBC viewers have been left unhappy with how the Winter Olympics in Italy are being covered this year.
